rulings.Case Title: EST. OF DOROTHY DONALDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) The examiner is unable to complete the review because this petition is missing information; not all sections have been filled out. Additionally, other required documents, such as the notice, proposed order, and duties and liabilities, are not on file. The Court will decide whether this petition should be dismissed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MALEE TANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/29/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service filed on 2/23/26 shows the incorrect title of the petition to be heard in item 1 of the Notice of Hearing. (2) It is unclear if paragraph 4 or paragraph 5 of the petition should be considered by the Court. (3) The number of shares of the securities held in Schwab account xx3607 were not set forth on Schedule E of the third amended first account filed on 8/12/25, so the number of shares shown on Schedule 1A cannot be confirmed. (4) The shares of Valley NTNL Banc at the beginning of this period should be 100,000, not 10,000. (5) The attorney fee declaration does not show the date services were provided. (Paragraph 11 of the petition requests $1,500 for attorney fees, not $1,200, but because the prayer requests $1,200 and the fee declaration shows $1,200, that will be the amount the Court ultimately orders.) (6) Assets transferred to the trust will be reviewed when the trust accounting petition is reviewed. (7) The conservator might wish to request fees now and defer payment until later. This Court does not favor fees being deferred indefinitely to be reviewed long after services were provided. Deadline to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any new filed papers is 4/15/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEANNE T. BUTTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/21/2026. The following issues to be cleared/addressed two weeks prior to the date: (1) Please clarify the cash value listed in the I&A. The order entered on 10/18/2023 in PCN-17-300528 approved a distribution of $82,469.12 to the personal representative, reflecting the cash balance after deducting approved fees, reimbursements, and reserve from $96,393.72. Yet, the I&A still reports the full $96,393.72. It appears the petitioner paid attorneys' fees and reimbursements out of pocket, rather than from conservatorship assets as directed, and now seeks reimbursement. Please explain this arrangement and update the fee base if necessary. (2) Please include the missing details for the $3,410.04 reimbursement request: payee, purpose, date, and amount. (3) The petition budgets $600 for post-petition costs. Please explain why the reserve cannot cover this expense. (4) Please clarify the reimbursement to counsel for advanced costs, as Paragraph 20 presents two different totals: $1,822.93 and $1,792.22. (5) The FDIC requirement remains unmet, as an heir's waiver does not negate it. The Court will determine. (6) The proposed order has not yet been submitted. The order must reflect the above issues.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/29/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. (2) Bond in the related case will not be reduced or waived until proof of the additional trustee bond is filed. (3) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] (4) It appears that the value of the assets received from the conservatorship estate were changed on Schedule 1C. (The accounting has not been reviewed in detail, as still no courtesy copy has been received by the examiner.) (5) The trustee might wish to request his compensation and defer payment until later of any fees ordered by the Court. (Paragraph 9 of the petition appears to be incomplete.) Deadline to provide the assigned examiner a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any other filed papers is 4/15/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ESPERANZA D. LOPEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/21/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) The report of status of administration must set forth the status of the estate, including, but not limited to, allegations regarding notice pursuant to Probate Code Section 9202, disclosure of all creditor claims and their dispositions, requests for special notice, and any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution. 2) All notices must have an attachment regarding remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 3) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and delivery courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 04/07/2026 to avoid additional continuance.
